summary: auth login --chrome also captures the publication's Creator-session cookie (connect.sid) from its custom domain, so Creator endpoints (drafts, scheduled_release, sections) work for custom-domain publications.
reason: Substack stores connect.sid on the publication's Creator host. For a custom-domain publication that is the custom domain (e.g. trevinsays.com), reached when {pub}.substack.com 301-redirects there; the .substack.com session cookie does not follow cross-domain, so Creator endpoints returned 403. resolveCreatorHost follows the redirect; captureCreatorDomainCookie writes connect.sid into the cookie jar scoped to that host, and the stdlib http.Client carries it across the 301 hop automatically — no per-endpoint routing change needed.
validated_outcome: With SUBSTACK_PUBLICATION set, auth login --chrome reports 'Captured Creator session for trevinsays.com'; `drafts list --data-source live` returns real drafts (HTTP 200, byline Trevin Chow) with zero 403s. Note: a session that 403s after expiry can leave a rotated duplicate connect.sid in the jar; clearing ~/.local/share/substack-pp-cli/cookies.json + re-running auth login --chrome restores a clean capture.
